articles.summary: As utilities struggle to keep pace with AI-driven demand, a new industry coalition aims to create a common playbook for powering next-generation data centers.

articles.headline: How Do Utilities Determine Which AI Data Centers Get Grid Access?
articles.summary: Planning documents from Georgia Power, Duke Energy, and Dominion Energy reveal how utilities filter AI demand, test scenarios, and plan investments.

articles.headline: Why AI Data Centers Make Existing Power Plants More Valuable
articles.summary: As AI accelerates electricity demand, utilities and investors are paying a growing premium for existing generation that can reach the grid faster than new projects.

articles.summary: The initiative’s success now hinges on whether it can scale faster than the mounting risks around energy, financing, governance, and community consent.

articles.headline: Rack-Based Environmental Monitoring: Benefits, Insights, and Getting Started
articles.summary: Rack-based environmental monitoring enhances data center efficiency by providing granular insights into temperature, humidity, and airflow.

articles.headline: CoreWeave Unveils Aria to Streamline AI Workflows for Data Centers
articles.summary: The cloud company’s new AI tool analyzes training experiments, visualizes data, and automates insights as cloud providers expand beyond infrastructure.

articles.headline: AI’s Duplicate Demand Problem Drives Grids to Commitment-First Planning
articles.summary: Recent FERC filings show AI developers and grid operators converging on stricter readiness rules to separate real power demand from speculative projects.

articles.summary: Oracle’s challenge to Wisconsin’s AI data center tariff could shape how regulators nationwide require financial guarantees for massive power projects.

articles.summary: Operators are deploying battery systems to support reliability and grid flexibility, but cost, performance, and complexity still hinder adoption.

articles.summary: Vultr selects HPE and Nvidia AI infrastructure as enterprise demand shifts from model training to production inference and large-scale deployments.
